questions.question: Poverty line in India is determined based on:
questions.explanation: India's poverty line is primarily based on minimum required calorie intake (2,400 kcal/day rural; 2,100 kcal/day urban) and the expenditure needed to achieve it. The Tendulkar Committee revised this methodology.

questions.question: Economics is defined as the study of:
options: How individuals, businesses, and governments allocate scarce resources to satisfy unlimited wants
questions.explanation: Economics (from Greek 'oikonomia' = household management): study of how scarce resources (land, labor, capital, enterprise) are allocated to satisfy unlimited human wants. Alfred Marshall: 'Science of material welfare.' Lionel Robbins: 'Science of choice under scarcity.'

questions.question: The National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA) guarantees:
options: 100 days of unskilled manual work per year to any rural household that demands it — minimum wage paid, unemployed allowance if work not provided within 15 days
questions.explanation: MGNREGA (2005, implemented 2006): provides legal right to 100 days of unskilled work per household per year at minimum wage. If work not provided within 15 days, unemployment allowance paid. Works include road construction, water conservation, land development. Among world's largest social protection programs. Renamed to include Gandhi: MGNREGS.

questions.question: The Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY) aims to provide:
options: Housing for All — affordable housing to urban and rural poor by 2022 (extended to 2024); includes PMAY-Urban and PMAY-Gramin
questions.explanation: PMAY (2015): targets housing for all urban slum dwellers and rural homeless. PMAY-Urban: credit-linked subsidy scheme for home loans (for EWS/LIG/MIG). PMAY-Gramin: provides Rs 1.2 lakh (plain areas) or Rs 1.3 lakh (NE, hilly) grant for rural house construction. Completed 3 crore+ houses.

questions.question: The National Rural Health Mission (NHM) was launched in:
options: 2005 — to improve healthcare in rural India; later merged with National Urban Health Mission to form NHM in 2013; ASHAs (Accredited Social Health Activists) are key workers
questions.explanation: NRHM (National Rural Health Mission, 2005): flagship health scheme for rural areas. Key feature: ASHA workers — trained female community health activists (one per 1000 population) who link communities with health facilities. NRHM + National Urban Health Mission merged into NHM (National Health Mission) in 2013.

questions.question: The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme was introduced to:
options: Incentivize companies to increase domestic manufacturing — financial incentives based on incremental sales; initially 13 sectors including mobile phones, pharmaceuticals, automobiles, textiles
questions.explanation: PLI Scheme (2020-21): government gives manufacturers financial incentives (3-5% of incremental production over a base year) to boost domestic manufacturing. Initially 13 sectors: mobile phones (Apple, Samsung set up factories), pharmaceuticals (API), automobiles, textiles, food processing, etc. Aims to make India a global manufacturing hub.

questions.question: The Atal Pension Yojana (APY) targets:
options: Unorganized sector workers — provides guaranteed pension of Rs 1,000-5,000/month at age 60; administered by PFRDA; co-contribution by government for eligible subscribers
questions.explanation: APY (2015, replaced Swavalamban scheme): for unorganized sector workers. Subscribers aged 18-40 contribute monthly; at 60, receive guaranteed pension of Rs 1,000/2,000/3,000/4,000/5,000 per month. Government (co-contribution of 50% of contribution or Rs 1,000/year, whichever lower) for eligible subscribers for 5 years. Administered by PFRDA (Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority).

questions.question: The UDAN (Ude Desh Ka Aam Nagrik) scheme is related to:
options: Regional Air Connectivity Scheme — makes air travel affordable on unserved/underserved routes; caps airfare for 1-hour flight; launched 2016
questions.explanation: UDAN (Ude Desh Ka Aam Nagrik — Let the Common Citizen Fly) 2016: provides affordable air connectivity to Tier-2/Tier-3 cities. Viability Gap Funding (VGF) provided to airlines operating on unserved routes. Capped airfare: Rs 2,500 for 1-hour flight. Revived and developed many regional airports. Over 400 routes operationalized.

questions.question: The AMRUT (Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation) scheme focuses on:
options: Basic urban infrastructure (water supply, sewerage, storm water, parks, non-motorized transport) for cities with 1 lakh+ population; 500 cities; 2015-2022 (AMRUT 2.0: 2021-26)
questions.explanation: AMRUT (2015, replaced JNNURM): provides every household with a tap with assured supply of water and a sewerage connection; parks and public spaces. 500 cities. AMRUT 2.0 (2021-26): 100% coverage of urban water supply, 100% sewerage management, rejuvenation of water bodies, and operational & maintenance reforms.

questions.question: The Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY scheme provides:
options: Health insurance of Rs 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalization to ~10 crore poor/vulnerable families (50 crore beneficiaries) — launched 2018
questions.explanation: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY, 2018): world's largest health assurance scheme. Covers Rs 5 lakh per family per year for hospitalization. Target: 10.74 crore poor/vulnerable families (50 crore beneficiaries) — bottom 40% of population. Cashless treatment at empanelled private and public hospitals. SECC 2011 data used for beneficiary identification.

questions.question: The One District One Product (ODOP) scheme promotes:
options: Each district of India to develop and market one unique product — integrates local crafts, agriculture, and industry into value chain; 761 products from 761 districts identified
questions.explanation: ODOP (2018, DPIIT under Ministry of Commerce): identifies and promotes one distinctive product per district. Products range from handicrafts (Pashmina, Kanjeevaram silk) to food products (Rasgulla of Kolkata, Darjeeling tea) to agricultural products. Creates a brand identity, links artisans/farmers to markets, and promotes exports.

questions.question: The Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan (SSA) was launched to:
options: Universalize elementary education (Classes 1-8) — free and compulsory education for all children 6-14 years; merged into Samagra Shiksha in 2018
questions.explanation: SSA (2001): flagship program for universalizing elementary education (RTE Act 2009 gave it constitutional backing). Established/upgraded schools in unserved habitations, recruited teachers, built classrooms. Mid-Day Meal Scheme complemented SSA. In 2018, SSA, RMSA (secondary), and Teacher Education merged into Samagra Shiksha Abhiyan.

questions.question: The National Pension System (NPS) replaced the Old Pension Scheme (OPS) for Central Government employees from:
options: January 1, 2004 (for employees joining on/after that date) — contributory scheme where employee contributes 10% and government 14% of basic+DA; managed by PFRDA
questions.explanation: NPS (National Pension System, 2004): replaced defined benefit OPS for central government employees joining from January 1, 2004 onwards. Contributory (employee: 10%, employer: 14% for central govt). Market-linked returns. Managed by PFRDA. NPS extended to all citizens in 2009 (Swavalamban/NPS-Lite for unorganized sector).

questions.question: The Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY) was launched to:
options: Provide all-weather road connectivity to unconnected habitations (500+ population in plain areas, 250+ in hilly/tribal areas) — launched 2000, PM Atal Bihari Vajpayee
questions.explanation: PMGSY (2000): flagship scheme to provide all-weather road connectivity to unconnected rural habitations. Target: 500+ population habitations in plain areas, 250+ in NE/hilly/tribal. World's largest rural road program. PMGSY Phase III (2019): upgrade 1.25 lakh km of existing rural roads. Funded 60% Centre: 40% State (90:10 for NE/special category).

questions.question: The 'poverty trap' is a situation where:
options: Poor people cannot escape poverty because they lack resources to invest in health, education, or productive assets — low income → low savings → low investment → low income (vicious cycle)
Poverty is self-fulfilling prophecy due to geography
questions.explanation: Poverty Trap (Jeffrey Sachs): poor people/countries stuck in poverty because: too poor to save/invest → capital doesn't accumulate → productivity stays low → income stays low. Solutions: 'Big Push' (massive external aid to break the cycle), conditional cash transfers, microfinance. Counter-view (Daron Acemoglu, James Robinson): institutions matter more than external shocks.

questions.question: The National Sample Survey (NSS) / PLFS (Periodic Labour Force Survey) provides data on:
options: Employment, unemployment, wages, living standards, consumer expenditure in India — PLFS replaced NSS on employment from 2017; annual survey with quarterly urban data
questions.explanation: NSO conducts: PLFS (Periodic Labour Force Survey, 2017): quarterly urban data + annual rural+urban data on employment, wages, migration patterns. HCES (Household Consumer Expenditure Survey): spending patterns (last conducted 2022-23 after gap since 2011-12 — 2017-18 data was withheld due to controversy). These surveys inform poverty estimates and policy.

questions.question: Which Five Year Plan was the most successful in India in terms of achieving its growth target?
options: Sixth Five Year Plan (1980-85) — achieved 5.7% vs target of 5.2% growth; under PM Indira Gandhi; focused on poverty alleviation, self-reliance; Jawahar Rozgar Yojana launched
questions.explanation: Sixth FYP (1980-85): India's most successful plan — exceeded its growth target. Focus: poverty alleviation, self-reliance, minimum needs program. Also notable: Third FYP (1961-66) was derailed by 1962 India-China war, 1965 India-Pakistan war, and drought → 'plan holiday' 1966-69. Eleventh FYP (2007-12) achieved 7.9% growth.

questions.question: The concept of 'disguised unemployment' is different from 'seasonal unemployment' in that:
options: Disguised unemployment: excess labor with zero marginal product (mainly in agriculture, year-round). Seasonal unemployment: workers unemployed during off-season (only part of year)
questions.explanation: Disguised Unemployment (structural): workers appear employed but contribute zero output (farm has 5 workers when 3 suffice). Zero marginal product. Removing 2 workers doesn't reduce output. Seasonal Unemployment: workers unemployed part of year (farmers during off-season, tourism workers in lean season). Cyclic unemployment: due to recession. Frictional: between jobs.

questions.question: The Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) scheme provides:
options: Package of 6 services to children 0-6 years and pregnant/lactating mothers: supplementary nutrition, immunization, health check-up, referral services, pre-school education, nutrition & health education
questions.explanation: ICDS (1975): world's largest and most comprehensive early childhood development program. 6 services through Anganwadi Centres: (1) Supplementary nutrition, (2) Immunization, (3) Health check-up, (4) Referral services, (5) Pre-school non-formal education (3-6 years), (6) Nutrition & health education (for women). 1.4 million Anganwadis, 14 crore beneficiaries.

questions.question: India's MUDRA loan scheme has three categories named after:
options: Three stages of business growth: Shishu (baby — up to Rs 50,000 for new/small businesses), Kishore (adolescent — Rs 50,000-5 lakh, for established micro units), Tarun (young adult — Rs 5-10 lakh, for medium micro units)
questions.explanation: MUDRA (Micro Units Development and Refinance Agency) loan categories: Shishu (Rs 50,000): startups, very small; Kishore (Rs 50k-5 lakh): established small businesses; Tarun (Rs 5-10 lakh): medium micro units. Tarun Plus (2024): up to Rs 20 lakh for Tarun category with good track record. All collateral-free. Interest rates typically 10-12%.

questions.question: The index 'Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI)' measures poverty using:
options: Three dimensions: health (nutrition, child mortality), education (years of schooling, school attendance), and living standards (cooking fuel, sanitation, water, electricity, housing, assets) — UNDP/OPHI
questions.explanation: MPI (UNDP and OPHI — Oxford Poverty and Human Development Initiative): multidimensional poverty measure using 10 indicators across 3 dimensions. A household is 'deprived' in each indicator; 'multidimensionally poor' if deprived in >33% of weighted indicators. India's MPI: 415 million exited poverty 2005-06 to 2019-21 (massive reduction). India publishes National MPI annually.

questions.question: The term 'terms of trade' in international economics refers to:
options: Ratio of export price index to import price index — if terms of trade improve (exports become relatively more expensive), country can import more with same exports; important for commodity exporters
questions.explanation: Terms of Trade (ToT) = (Export Price Index / Import Price Index) × 100. If ToT rises: country's exports buy more imports → welfare improves. If ToT falls: exports buy fewer imports → welfare falls. Important for developing countries exporting primary commodities (prices volatile, tend to fall relative to manufactured goods — Prebisch-Singer hypothesis). India: ToT typically negative due to oil imports.

questions.question: India's 'One Nation One Ration Card' (ONORC) scheme allows:
options: PDS (ration card) beneficiaries to claim their subsidized food entitlement from any Fair Price Shop in India — especially beneficial for migrant workers who move to different states for work
questions.explanation: ONORC (2019-2021, nationwide rollout): using Aadhaar-linked biometric authentication at FPS (Fair Price Shop), beneficiary can claim PDS quota from any FPS in India. Critical for: migrant workers (can claim entitlement at destination state), seasonal migrants. All 36 states/UTs joined ONORC by 2021. 80+ crore beneficiaries. Managed through Integrated Management of PDS (IM-PDS) portal.

questions.question: The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) provides:
options: Rs 945 crore seed funding to early-stage startups through incubators — for proof of concept, prototype development, trials, market entry; Max Rs 20 lakh grant or Rs 50 lakh soft loan per startup
questions.explanation: SISFS (2021-25): Rs 945 crore over 4 years. Grants through selected incubators: Rs 20 lakh for proof of concept/validation (PoC, prototype, trials); Rs 50 lakh as soft loan for commercialization/market entry. Incubators apply to DPIIT; approved incubators select startups. Fills the gap between angel investment and formal VC: 'seed funding' stage.

questions.question: Frictional unemployment occurs when:
options: Workers are between jobs or searching for better jobs
Jobs exist but skills don't match
questions.explanation: Frictional unemployment: temporary unemployment while workers transition between jobs or search for better ones. It's natural and exists even in a healthy economy (part of 'natural rate of unemployment').

questions.question: The Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS) in India is conducted by:
questions.explanation: PLFS is conducted by NSO (National Statistical Office) under MoSPI. Launched 2017-18, provides quarterly urban + annual rural employment/unemployment estimates.

questions.question: India's Urban Employment Guarantee Scheme (UGEGS) for urban poor was introduced in:
options: 2020 during COVID-19 pandemic as 'PM Garib Kalyan Rojgar Abhiyan' for migrant workers — some states like Rajasthan have their own urban employment guarantee schemes
questions.explanation: Urban employment guarantee: the PM Garib Kalyan Rojgar Abhiyan (June 2020) provided 125 days of work to returning migrant workers in 116 districts. Rajasthan's Indira Gandhi Shahri Rozgar Guarantee Yojana (2022): 100 days urban employment. Kerala has similar scheme. No national-level urban employment guarantee act yet.

questions.question: POSHAN 2.0 (2021) merged which earlier schemes?
options: POSHAN Abhiyaan and Supplementary Nutrition Programme and other nutrition schemes
questions.explanation: POSHAN 2.0: merged POSHAN Abhiyaan, ICDS (Supplementary Nutrition), National Creche Scheme, Kishori Shakti Yojana into an integrated nutrition support programme under Mission Saksham Anganwadi.

questions.question: PM POSHAN scheme (renamed from Mid-Day Meal in 2021) covers students up to:
questions.explanation: PM POSHAN: covers students from pre-primary (Balvatika) to Class VIII in government and government-aided schools. ~11.8 crore students across ~11.2 lakh schools.

questions.question: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) is the flagship scheme for:
options: Short-term skill training and certification for youth
questions.explanation: PMKVY (2015): short-term skill training by NSDC through Training Partners. Provides free training, assessment, certification, and monetary reward. Target: skill 1 crore people.

questions.question: The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme provides incentive as a percentage of:
options: Incremental sales over a base year
questions.explanation: PLI: companies receive incentive (4-20% depending on sector) on incremental sales above their base year (2019-20). Covers 14 sectors. Aims to attract investment, enhance manufacturing, and boost exports.

questions.question: Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY (Jan Arogya Yojana) provides health cover of how much per family per year?
questions.explanation: PM-JAY (launched September 23, 2018): Rs 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization. World's largest government health insurance scheme. ~55 crore beneficiaries (50 crore poor).

questions.question: Which committee recommended the rationalization of centrally sponsored schemes (CSS) in India?
questions.explanation: B.K. Chaturvedi Committee (2011): recommended rationalizing and restructuring CSS. Later, Sub-Group of CMs under CM Shivraj Singh Chouhan (2015) recommended reducing CSS from 66 to 28 for more flexibility.

questions.question: Which organization replaced the Planning Commission of India in 2015?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog replaced the Planning Commission in 2015 to foster cooperative federalism and promote economic growth.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act?
questions.explanation: The primary goal of MGNREGA is to generate employment for rural laborers.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes is implemented by the Tamil Nadu government to support farmers?
questions.explanation: Uzhavar Pathukappu Thittam is a scheme implemented by the Tamil Nadu government to support farmers.

questions.question: What is the term for the total value of goods and services produced within a country's borders?
questions.explanation: GDP stands for Gross Domestic Product, which is the total value of goods and services produced within a country.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a developing economy like India?
questions.explanation: A developing economy like India is often characterized by a significant dependence on agriculture.

questions.question: What is the name of the flagship program launched by the Government of India to alleviate poverty?
questions.explanation: Swarnjayanti Gram Swarozgar Yojana is a flagship program launched to alleviate poverty.

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of agricultural system practiced in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Delta farming is a type of agricultural system practiced in the delta regions of Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: What is the primary focus of the National Food Security Mission?
questions.explanation: The primary focus of the National Food Security Mission is to increase food grain production.

questions.question: Which of the following is a welfare scheme implemented by the Government of Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: All of the above options are welfare schemes implemented by the Government of Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: What is the name of the economic planning body that was replaced by NITI Aayog in 2015?
questions.explanation: The Planning Commission was replaced by NITI Aayog in 2015.

questions.question: Which of the following is a poverty alleviation program launched by the Government of India?
questions.explanation: Swarnjayanti Gram Swarozgar Yojana is a poverty alleviation program launched by the Government of India.

questions.question: What is the name of the think tank that replaced the Planning Commission of India?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog is the think tank that replaced the Planning Commission of India.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key feature of the Tamil Nadu government's budgeting process?
questions.explanation: Outcome-based budgeting is a key feature of the Tamil Nadu government's budgeting process.

questions.question: What is the term for the difference between the value of goods exported and imported by a country?
questions.explanation: Trade deficit occurs when the value of goods imported exceeds the value of goods exported.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes is aimed at providing financial assistance to small and marginal farmers in Tamil Nadu?
options: Tamil Nadu Agricultural Labourers Welfare Scheme
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu Agricultural Labourers Welfare Scheme provides financial assistance to small and marginal farmers.

questions.question: What is the name of the national mission launched by the Government of India to improve agricultural productivity?
questions.explanation: National Mission for Sustainable Agriculture aims to improve agricultural productivity.

questions.question: Which organization replaced the Planning Commission of India?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog replaced the Planning Commission of India in 2015. It is the policy think tank for the Government of India.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key feature of the Tamil Nadu government's poverty reduction strategy?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu government has implemented various schemes to reduce poverty, including subsidy on fertilizers, free rice scheme, and mid-day meal scheme.

questions.question: What is the name of the scheme launched by the Government of India to provide financial assistance to the poor?
questions.explanation: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Antyodaya Yojana is a scheme launched by the Government of India to provide financial assistance to the poor.

questions.question: Which of the following is a measure of poverty?
questions.explanation: Headcount ratio is a measure of poverty, which is the percentage of population living below the poverty line.

questions.question: What is the name of the economic planning body that was replaced by NITI Aayog?
questions.explanation: The Planning Commission was replaced by NITI Aayog in 2015.

questions.question: Which state has the highest GDP in India?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra has the highest GDP in India, accounting for about 15% of the country's GDP.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the Public Distribution System in India?
questions.explanation: The main objective of the Public Distribution System is to ensure food security by providing subsidized foodgrains to the poor.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes is aimed at providing financial assistance to farmers?
questions.explanation: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana is a scheme aimed at providing financial assistance to farmers in case of crop failure.

questions.question: What is the name of the flagship program of the Government of India to promote inclusive growth?
questions.explanation: Sabka Saath Sabka Vikas is the flagship program of the Government of India to promote inclusive growth and development.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key indicator of poverty?
questions.explanation: Headcount ratio measures the percentage of population living below the poverty line. It is a key indicator of poverty.

questions.question: What is the name of the poverty reduction scheme launched by the Tamil Nadu government?
questions.explanation: Pudhu Vazhvu Thittam is a poverty reduction scheme launched by the Tamil Nadu government. It aims to reduce poverty and improve livelihoods.

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of agricultural system in India?
questions.explanation: Green Revolution is a type of agricultural system in India. It aimed to increase food production and reduce poverty.

questions.question: Which of the following is a measure of national income?
questions.explanation: GDP is a measure of national income. It represents the total value of goods and services produced within a country.

questions.question: What is the name of the budgeting scheme launched by the Government of India?
questions.explanation: Zero-based budgeting is a budgeting scheme launched by the Government of India. It aims to optimize resource allocation.

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of international trade agreement?
questions.explanation: WTO is a type of international trade agreement. It aims to promote free trade and economic cooperation among member countries.

questions.question: What is the name of the economic scheme launched by the Government of Tamil Nadu to promote GDP growth?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu Vision 2023 is an economic scheme launched by the Government of Tamil Nadu. It aims to promote GDP growth and development.

questions.question: What is the term for the difference between the value of exports and imports of a country?
questions.explanation: The balance of trade refers to the difference between the value of exports and imports.

questions.question: What is the name of the Indian government's flagship program to provide financial inclusion?
questions.explanation: Pradhan Mantri Jan-Dhan Yojana is the Indian government's flagship program to provide financial inclusion.

questions.question: What is the term for the total value of goods and services produced within a country?
questions.explanation: GDP refers to the total value of goods and services produced within a country.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes is aimed at providing nutrition to pregnant women and lactating mothers?
questions.explanation: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana is aimed at providing nutrition to pregnant women and lactating mothers.

questions.question: What is the name of the Tamil Nadu government's scheme to provide financial assistance to the poor?
questions.explanation: Kalaignar Insurance Scheme is a scheme by the Tamil Nadu government to provide financial assistance to the poor.

questions.question: Which organization replaced the Planning Commission in India?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog replaced the Planning Commission in 2015, aiming to promote cooperative federalism.

questions.question: What is the name of the commission that estimates the poverty line in India?
questions.explanation: The Suresh Tendulkar Committee estimated the poverty line in India in 2009.

questions.question: Which of the following is a welfare scheme implemented by the Government of India?
questions.explanation: All of the above are welfare schemes implemented by the Government of India.

questions.question: Which of the following is a budgeting concept that aims to allocate resources based on priorities?
questions.explanation: Zero-based budgeting is a concept that aims to allocate resources based on priorities.

questions.question: What is the name of the economic indicator that measures the total income earned by citizens of a country?
questions.explanation: GNP stands for Gross National Product, which measures the total income earned by citizens of a country.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA)?
questions.explanation: MGNREGA aims to provide guaranteed employment to rural laborers, thereby supporting their livelihood.

questions.question: Which committee is responsible for estimating poverty lines in India?
questions.explanation: The Rangarajan Committee estimated poverty lines in India and submitted its report in 2014.

questions.question: What is the name of the scheme launched by the Government of Tamil Nadu to provide financial assistance to the poor?
questions.explanation: Kalaignar Kappeettu Thittam is a scheme launched by the Government of Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Which of the following is a method to calculate national income?
questions.explanation: Value Added Method is one of the methods to calculate national income.

questions.question: What is the name of the committee that fixes the poverty line in India?
questions.explanation: Suresh Tendulkar Committee is known for fixing the poverty line in India.

questions.question: Which institution replaced the Planning Commission of India in 2015?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog replaced the Planning Commission in 2015 to foster cooperative federalism and promote economic growth.

questions.question: What is the term for the difference between the total value of exports and imports of a country?
questions.explanation: Balance of trade refers to the difference between exports and imports.

questions.question: Which of the following is a measure of poverty in India?
questions.explanation: All the above options are measures of poverty in India.

questions.question: What is the name of the flagship program of the Government of India to achieve universal health coverage?
questions.explanation: Ayushman Bharat is the flagship program for universal health coverage.

questions.question: Which of the following is a component of the Tamil Nadu government's budget?
questions.explanation: All the above options are components of the Tamil Nadu government's budget.

questions.question: What is the name of the economic system in which the government plays a significant role in the allocation of resources?
questions.explanation: Mixed economy is the system where the government plays a significant role.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the Indian economy?
questions.explanation: Agriculture is a significant sector in the Indian economy.

questions.question: Which of the following committees is responsible for estimating poverty in India?
questions.explanation: The Rangarajan Committee is responsible for estimating poverty in India.

questions.question: What is the name of the scheme launched by the Tamil Nadu government to provide financial assistance to poor families?
questions.explanation: Kalaignar Insurance Scheme provides financial assistance to poor families.

questions.question: Which of the following is a major challenge in implementing poverty reduction programs in India?
questions.explanation: All the options are major challenges in implementing poverty reduction programs.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key indicator of poverty in India?
questions.explanation: Headcount ratio is a key indicator of poverty in India.

questions.question: What is the name of the scheme launched by the Government of India to provide financial inclusion to the poor?
options: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana
questions.explanation: Pradhan Mantri Jan-Dhan Yojana aims at providing financial inclusion to the poor.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key feature of the Tamil Nadu government's welfare scheme?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu is known for its free rice distribution scheme.

questions.question: What is the term for the difference between the value of exports and imports in India's international trade?
questions.explanation: Trade deficit occurs when the value of imports exceeds exports.

questions.question: Which economic system is characterized by collective farming and state-controlled distribution of resources?
questions.explanation: Communism is an economic system with collective farming and state control.

questions.question: What is the name of the Indian government's program aimed at providing financial inclusion to the poor?
questions.explanation: Jan Dhan Yojana aims to provide financial inclusion to the poor.

questions.question: Which of the following is a measure of the overall economic activity in a region like Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: GDP measures the overall economic activity in a region.

questions.question: What is the term for government spending that is financed by borrowing rather than taxation?
questions.explanation: Deficit financing is government spending financed by borrowing.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes is aimed at providing social security to the elderly in Tamil Nadu?
options: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme provides social security to the elderly.

questions.question: What is the name of the Indian government's think tank that replaced the Planning Commission?
options: National Institute of Public Finance and Policy
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog is the think tank that replaced the Planning Commission.

questions.question: What is the name of the poverty estimation method used by the Planning Commission of India?
questions.explanation: The Tendulkar committee method is used to estimate poverty in India.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of the Indian economy?
questions.explanation: India has a mixed economy with both public and private sectors.

questions.question: What is the name of the flagship program of the Government of India to promote agricultural development?
questions.explanation: Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana is a flagship program to promote agricultural development.

questions.question: Which of the following is a welfare scheme implemented by the Tamil Nadu government?
questions.explanation: All of the above are welfare schemes implemented by the Tamil Nadu government.

questions.question: What is the name of the economic think tank that replaced the Planning Commission of India?
options: National Institute of Public Finance and Policy
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog is the replacement of Planning Commission of India.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the Tamil Nadu economy?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu has a high services sector growth rate.

questions.question: What is the name of the national poverty reduction program launched by the Government of India?
questions.explanation: National Rural Livelihood Mission is a national poverty reduction program.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act?

questions.question: What is the name of the national income concept that includes the value of goods and services produced within a country's borders?
questions.explanation: GDP measures the value of goods and services produced within a country.

questions.question: Which of the following is a budgeting approach used by the Government of Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Outcome-based budgeting is used by the Government of Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: What is the name of the economic concept that measures the total value of goods and services produced by a country's citizens, regardless of where they are produced?
questions.explanation: GNP measures the total value of goods and services produced by a country's citizens.

questions.question: Which committee is responsible for estimating India's national income?
questions.explanation: The Central Statistical Office is responsible for estimating national income.

questions.question: What is the name of the poverty reduction program launched by the Government of India in 1978?
questions.explanation: Integrated Rural Development Programme aims to provide assistance to rural poor.

questions.question: What is the name of the economic planning approach adopted by India from 1951 to 2014?
questions.explanation: Five-Year Plans were adopted by India for economic planning and development.

questions.question: What is the name of the think tank that replaced the Planning Commission's 'Planning Body' in 2015?
options: National Institute of Public Finance and Policy
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog serves as the think tank for the Government of India, providing policy and economic advice.

questions.question: Which of the following is a component of the Gross Domestic Product of a country?
questions.explanation: National income is a component of the Gross Domestic Product.

questions.question: What is the name of the international trade agreement signed by India in 1995?
questions.explanation: India signed the WTO agreement in 1995.

questions.question: What is the term for the difference between the revenue receipts and revenue expenditure of a government?
questions.explanation: Revenue deficit is the difference between revenue receipts and revenue expenditure.

questions.question: What is the name of the committee that estimated poverty in India in 2012?
questions.explanation: The Rangarajan committee estimated poverty in India in 2012.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act, launched in 2006?
questions.explanation: The act aims to provide guaranteed employment to rural laborers. It focuses on employment generation.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes was launched by the Tamil Nadu government to provide financial assistance to the poor?
questions.explanation: Kalaignar Kappeettu Thittam is a scheme launched by the Tamil Nadu government to provide financial assistance to the poor.

questions.question: What is the name of the committee set up by the Planning Commission in 1979 to estimate poverty?
questions.explanation: The Lakdawala Committee was set up in 1979 to estimate poverty.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of the Special Economic Zones (SEZs) in India?
questions.explanation: SEZs in India are characterized by tax exemptions, subsidies on exports, and low labor costs.

questions.question: What is the name of the flagship program launched by the Government of India to address the issue of rural poverty?
questions.explanation: Swarnjayanti Gram Swarozgar Yojana is a flagship program launched to address rural poverty.

questions.question: Which of the following organizations replaced the Planning Commission of India in 2015?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog replaced the Planning Commission of India in 2015.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the Public Distribution System (PDS) in India?
questions.explanation: The primary objective of PDS is to ensure food security.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes is aimed at providing financial inclusion to the poor in Tamil Nadu?
options: Tamil Nadu Government's Financial Inclusion Scheme
questions.explanation: Pradhan Mantri Jan-Dhan Yojana aims to provide financial inclusion to the poor.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the Tamil Nadu government's budgeting process?
questions.explanation: Outcome-based budgeting is a feature of the Tamil Nadu government's budgeting process.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of the Tamil Nadu government's 'Pudhu Vazhvu' scheme?
questions.explanation: Pudhu Vazhvu scheme aims to reduce poverty in rural Tamil Nadu. It focuses on livelihood enhancement and social protection.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key feature of the Indian economy as per the NITI Aayog's Three-Year Action Agenda?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog's agenda emphasizes fiscal federalism for cooperative federalism. It promotes cooperation between center and states.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes is implemented by the Tamil Nadu government to provide financial assistance to poor families?
questions.explanation: Kalaignar Kappeettu Thittam provides financial assistance to poor families. It is a part of the state's poverty reduction initiatives.

questions.question: What is the name of the national mission launched by the Government of India to improve agricultural productivity and farmers' income?
questions.explanation: Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana aims to improve agricultural productivity. It was launched to increase farmers' income and improve rural livelihoods.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key indicator used to measure poverty in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Headcount ratio is used to measure poverty. It indicates the percentage of population living below the poverty line.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of the Tamil Nadu government's 'Pudhu Vazhvu' scheme, launched in 2011, in relation to poverty reduction and welfare?
questions.explanation: Pudhu Vazhvu scheme focuses on rural employment and poverty reduction. It aims to improve livelihoods of rural poor.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct sequence of poverty estimation committees in India?
questions.explanation: The Lakdawala Committee (1993) was followed by the Tendulkar Committee (2009), and then the Rangarajan Committee (2014).

questions.question: Under the National Food Security Act (NFSA), 2013, what is the monthly entitlement of food grains per person for priority households?
questions.explanation: The NFSA provides 5 kg of food grains per person per month to priority households at subsidized rates.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government scheme provides financial assistance of ₹1,000 per month to women heads of families?
options: D. Tamil Nadu Women Empowerment Scheme
questions.explanation: The Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thittam, launched in 2023, provides ₹1,000 monthly assistance to eligible women heads of families.

questions.question: As per the latest NITI Aayog's Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI), which Indian state has the highest percentage of population living in multidimensional poverty?
questions.explanation: Bihar has the highest percentage of population in multidimensional poverty, followed by Jharkhand and Uttar Pradesh.

questions.question: The 'Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana (PMGKAY)' was initially launched in response to which event?
questions.explanation: PMGKAY was launched in March 2020 as a relief measure to provide free food grains to poor households during the COVID-19 pandemic.

questions.question: In Tamil Nadu, the 'Pudhumai Penn Scheme' provides financial assistance to which category of beneficiaries?
options: B. Girl children from poor families
questions.explanation: The Pudhumai Penn Scheme provides ₹1,000 per month to girl children from poor families who studied from Class 6 to 12 without a break.
